title: anaphalioides keriensis
intro: Anaphalioides keriensis, also known as the New Zealand broom or Keri broom, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and narrow, linear leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Anaphalioides keriensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and support immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds, flu, and other infections. Herbal preparations of Anaphalioides keriensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made from the leaves and flowers of the plant.
